metadata.title: The Photoelectric Effect
metadata.notes: Wonder-register variant of vox-photoelectric-effect. Medhavy palette (Okabe-Ito). Voice: af_kore.
metadata.description_blurb: In 1887 Heinrich Hertz proved light is a wave — and noticed, the same year, the experiment that would break the wave theory. Three impossible facts, one patent clerk, and the equation that made light come apart in chunks.

beats.narration_text: What if a single experiment forced physicists to abandon everything they knew about light? UV light hits metal — and out come electrons. Three things happen that waves simply cannot explain. Let's find out why.
beats.tts_normalized_text: What if a single experiment forced physicists to abandon everything they knew about light? UV light hits metal — and out come electrons. Three things happen that waves simply cannot explain. Let's find out why.

props.command: Why does UV light kick electrons out of metal when red light can't — no matter how bright it is? What does this tell us about the nature of light?
output: brighter light: more electrons, not faster ones
Einstein 1905: light arrives in whole packets

beats.narration_text: In 1887, Heinrich Hertz proved light is a wave — the crowning triumph of classical physics. The same year, he noticed something that would destroy it.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In 1887, Heinrich Hertz proved light is a wave — the crowning triumph of classical physics. The same year, he noticed something that would destroy it.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Hertz (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Heinrich Hertz portrait or spark-gap apparatus, 1880s; set shot.focus on his face / the gap

beats.narration_text: Ultraviolet light strikes metal and knocks electrons loose. Simple to observe, impossible to explain. This is the experiment that forced physics to start over.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Ultraviolet light strikes metal and knocks electrons loose. Simple to observe, impossible to explain. This is the experiment that forced physics to start over.

card.title: THE PHOTOELECTRIC EFFECT
card.sub: how light came apart in chunks

beats.narration_text: By 1902, Philipp Lenard had shown the sparks were electrons. By 1914, Robert Millikan had measured every feature of them. The data said three impossible things.
beats.tts_normalized_text: By 1902, Philipp Lenard had shown the sparks were electrons. By 1914, Robert Millikan had measured every feature of them. The data said three impossible things.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Lenard (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Philipp Lenard at apparatus, or a cathode-ray-era laboratory, circa 1900

beats.narration_text: Fact one: below a threshold frequency, nothing at all. A blinding red arc lamp frees no electrons from sodium. A dim ultraviolet lamp frees them instantly.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Fact one: below a threshold frequency, nothing at all. A blinding red arc lamp frees no electrons from sodium. A dim ultraviolet lamp frees them instantly.

shot.note: AI-generated plate — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO+ANNOTATION] carbon arc lamp / searchlight plate; annotation plane adds 'RED: nothing' vs 'UV: instantly' chips at assembly — plate slots now

beats.narration_text: Fact two: brighter light means more electrons — but not faster ones. Double the intensity, and each electron still carries exactly the same energy as before.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Fact two: brighter light means more electrons — but not faster ones. Double the intensity, and each electron still carries exactly the same energy as before.

viz.data: isotype: one row of electron squares, then double brightness -> two rows, every square the SAME size; 'same energy each' chip
viz.note: KE independent of intensity (chapter fact two)

beats.narration_text: Fact three: no delay. Emission begins within nanoseconds at any brightness above the threshold. Even the dimmest lamp never needs time to charge up an electron.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Fact three: no delay. Emission begins within nanoseconds at any brightness above the threshold. Even the dimmest lamp never needs time to charge up an electron.

shot.note: AI-generated plate — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] gold-leaf electroscope or precision lab instrument, circa 1900; set shot.focus on the instrument

beats.narration_text: Here's what's actually strange. A wave delivers energy continuously, spread everywhere at once. Given enough time, any color should slowly shake an electron loose. But none of this happens.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Here's what's actually strange. A wave delivers energy continuously, spread everywhere at once. Given enough time, any color should slowly shake an electron loose. But none of this happens.

viz.data: three classical expectations as serif labels — 'any color, given time' / 'brighter means faster' / 'energy needs to accumulate' — each gets a hand-drawn terracotta strike as the narration lands
viz.note: one editor's-pen voice per graphic: the strikes ARE the voice here

beats.narration_text: Enter a twenty-six-year-old patent clerk in Bern, in his miracle year: Albert Einstein, taking the step Planck himself refused.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Enter a twenty-six-year-old patent clerk in Bern, in his miracle year: Albert Einstein, taking the step Planck himself refused.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Einstein (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Einstein at the Bern patent office era (~1905, young, seated at desk); set shot.focus on his face

beats.narration_text: Light is not a continuous wave spreading through space. It arrives in discrete, whole packets — Planck's quanta, now applied to light itself. Einstein named them light quanta. We call them photons.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Light is not a continuous wave spreading through space. It arrives in discrete, whole packets — Planck's quanta, now applied to light itself. Einstein named them light quanta. We call them photons.

viz.data: a smooth navy wave greys out; a stream of discrete terracotta dots (photons) crosses in its place; 'Lichtquanten' serif label with hairline
viz.note: Einstein 1905: quantizes the FIELD itself — the step beyond Planck (chapter)

beats.narration_text: The interaction becomes beautifully simple. One photon meets one electron — a complete, indivisible event. Whole packet or nothing at all. A photon cannot pay in installments.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The interaction becomes beautifully simple. One photon meets one electron — a complete, indivisible event. Whole packet or nothing at all. A photon cannot pay in installments.

viz.data: one terracotta photon dot arrives at a slate metal edge, one navy electron leaves; a second sub-threshold photon fades with an X — whole packet or nothing

beats.narration_text: One equation captures all of it: the electron's kinetic energy equals the photon's energy minus the work function — the metal's exit fee. Each of those three impossible facts drops out immediately.
beats.tts_normalized_text: One equation captures all of it: the electron's kinetic energy equals the photon's energy minus the work function — the metal's exit fee. Each of those three impossible facts drops out immediately.

tangent.rhs: The photon's payment, minus the metal's exit fee.
tangent.claim: Exactly this — each photon acts alone, whole or not at all.

example.scenario: UV at 300 nm on sodium. Fee: 2.28 eV.
example.verdict: UV: electron keeps 1.85 eV · green: 0.01 short — nothing
example.cost: Sub-threshold photons never add up. Each acts alone.

tangent.reentry: And that is the whole law. Back to the three facts.

beats.narration_text: K-max: the maximum kinetic energy of the emitted electron. h-nu: the energy carried by one photon, set entirely by its frequency. Phi: the work function — the minimum energy the metal demands before it will release an electron.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Kay-max: the maximum kinetic energy of the emitted electron. H-new: the energy carried by one photon, set entirely by its frequency. Fie: the work function — the minimum energy the metal demands before it will release an electron.

beats.narration_text: Ultraviolet on sodium: the photon pays 4.13 electron volts, the exit fee is 2.28 — the electron keeps 1.85. Green light pays only 2.27. One hundredth of an electron volt short. Nothing escapes. Ever.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Ultraviolet on sodium: the photon pays 4.13 electron volts, the exit fee is 2.28 — the electron keeps 1.85. Green light pays only 2.27. One hundredth of an electron volt short. Nothing escapes. Ever.

beats.narration_text: Now the three impossible facts resolve. Threshold exists because no photon below the fee can pay. Faster electrons from bluer light because frequency sets the payment. No delay because one photon is one complete transaction — instantaneous.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Now the three impossible facts resolve. Threshold exists because no photon below the fee can pay. Faster electrons from bluer light because frequency sets the payment. No delay because one photon is one complete transaction — instantaneous.

viz.data: the three impossible facts return as serif labels, each now getting a navy check on its narration cue — the mirror of B07's strikes

beats.narration_text: Robert Millikan thought photons were absurd. He spent years trying to prove Einstein wrong — freshly scraped metal surfaces, vacuum, exquisite care.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Robert Millikan thought photons were absurd. He spent years trying to prove Einstein wrong — freshly scraped metal surfaces, vacuum, exquisite care.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Millikan (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Robert Millikan portrait or at apparatus (Caltech-era acceptable); set shot.focus on his face

beats.narration_text: Every metal gave the same slope: h over e. Einstein's equation fit perfectly. Millikan was forced to publish his own defeat — writing, "in spite of my personal conviction."
beats.tts_normalized_text: Every metal gave the same slope: h over e. Einstein's equation fit perfectly. Millikan was forced to publish his own defeat — writing, "in spite of my personal conviction."

viz.data: axes: stopping potential vs frequency; three parallel navy lines (sodium, lithium, potassium) draw on with different thresholds, one crimson slope label 'h/e — the same for every metal'

beats.narration_text: Einstein's Nobel Prize arrived in 1921 — for the photoelectric effect, not for relativity. Millikan's came two years later, for the very measurements he had designed to kill Einstein's theory.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Einstein's Nobel Prize arrived in 1921 — for the photoelectric effect, not for relativity. Millikan's came two years later, for the very measurements he had designed to kill Einstein's theory.

viz.data: two slate cards: EINSTEIN · 1921 · 'for the photoelectric effect' / MILLIKAN · 1923 · 'for measuring h' — terracotta ring on 'not relativity' line
viz.note: Einstein Nobel 1921 for photoelectric effect; Millikan 1923 (chapter)

beats.narration_text: Who was this patent clerk who broke classical physics? That is a story of its own. What he showed — that light is wave AND particle at once — still has no classical answer.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Who was this patent clerk who broke classical physics? That is a story of its own. What he showed — that light is wave AND particle at once — still has no classical answer.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Einstein (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Einstein portrait ~1905 (young, not the elderly icon); name label + dates at assembly; bio kicker per SKILL.md
